name: linear-cli
description: Linear CLI (schpet/linear-cli) — manage Linear.app issues, projects, milestones, cycles, initiatives, labels, documents, and teams from the terminal, with git/jj integration and JSON output for agents.

prerequisites.name: deno
prerequisites.install_hint: Install Deno from https://deno.land (winget install DenoLand.Deno) — used to install the linear CLI from JSR.
prerequisites.note: Only needed for the deno/JSR install method. Homebrew, npm, and prebuilt binaries are alternatives.

install.notes: The deno method installs to ~/.deno/bin (Windows: C:\Users\<you>\.deno\bin) — ensure that directory is on PATH. After install, authenticate with 'linear auth login'.

integration_test.description: linear must be authenticated — run 'linear auth login' with an API key from https://linear.app/settings/account/security if not

post_install_hints: Authenticate: run 'linear auth login' and paste an API key from https://linear.app/settings/account/security (stored in the system keyring).
Set project defaults: run 'linear config' in a repo to write .linear.toml (team_id, workspace, vcs).

If 'linear' is not found after install, persist the Deno bin dir to PATH — deno install never edits PATH. Windows (User PATH registry): powershell -NoProfile -Command "$p=[Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable('Path','User'); if($p -notlike '*\.deno\bin*'){[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable('Path',$p.TrimEnd(';')+';'+$env:USERPROFILE+'\.deno\bin','User')}". macOS/Linux: add 'export PATH="$HOME/.deno/bin:$PATH"' to ~/.zshrc or ~/.bashrc.
